summary: In this extremely brief post, Hotz draws a stark contrast between conventional life paths (having children) and his own chosen path (building technology). The post distills his entire philosophy of prioritizing creation and innovation over traditional milestones into just six words. It's a declaration of identity and values, suggesting that his legacy will be his technological contributions rather than biological offspring.
key_points: Traditional life milestones like having children are not the only valid path
Creating technology is presented as an equivalent or superior form of creation/legacy
Personal identity is defined by what you build, not conventional markers of success
money_quotes: Some people make babby. I make computer.
key_insight: Your legacy is defined by what you create, whether that's children or computers—Hotz has made his choice.
